vulnerability.reason: Generated mentions fine-tuning and injected canaries but describes lack of pipeline detection causing forced memorization, while reference describes fine-tuning reactivating pre-training memorization; related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Fine-tuning reactivates pre-training memorization, leaking injected canaries.
vulnerability.generated: Fine-tuning pipelines lack detection and removal of injected canary-trap backdoors, leaving models susceptible to forced memorization and data leakage.

impact.reason: Reference states legal and financial penalties for fine-tuning organizations; generated describes intellectual property theft and privacy violations for data owners and users – neither affected party nor damage type match.
impact.reference: Legal and financial penalties for fine-tuning organizations.
impact.generated: Proprietary fine-tuning data leaks from compromised models, causing intellectual property theft and privacy violations for data owners and their users.

dose_response.reason: Reference arc: pre-training privacy backdoors reactivate during fine-tuning, leaking canaries and leading to legal penalties. Generated arc: data provenance techniques enable attacks, exploit undetected backdoors to force memorization, leak proprietary data causing theft/privacy violations. Causal logic differs entirely.
dose_response.reference: Privacy backdoors in pre-trained models reactivate during fine-tuning, leaking canaries when probed, which exposes fine-tuning organizations to legal penalties even without wrongdoing.
dose_response.generated: Canary trap-based data provenance techniques enable privacy backdoor attacks that, through organizations fine-tuning pre-trained models on proprietary data, exploit undetected canary-trap backdoors in fine-tuning pipelines, forcing memorization that leaks proprietary data and causes intellectual property theft and privacy violations.
